title: pavonia speciosa var. hostmannii
intro: Pavonia speciosa var. hostmannii, also known as the royal rose mallow, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the mallow family (Malvaceae). Native to Mexico and parts of Central America, it has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiviral activities. Herbal preparations of Pavonia speciosa var. hostmannii can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various conditions, including wounds, fever, and digestive issues.
